user_query:
I'm trying to standardize the way I collect lead details and clean up my follow‑up emails. Please write a local script (any reasonable language) that:

1) Parses every .txt file in input/inbox/ to extract these fields and normalize them, then writes consolidated results to both outputs/applications.csv and outputs/applications.jsonl.
   - Input keys to read (case-insensitive, exact labels as shown in the files):
     Name:, Email:, Phone:, Loan purpose:, Property state:, Estimated credit score:, Loan amount:
   - Output columns/fields (in this order): source_file, customer_name, email, phone, loan_purpose, property_state, credit_score, loan_amount, missing_fields
   - Normalization rules:
     • loan_amount: strip currency symbols and commas, output as an integer number of dollars (e.g., "$450,000" -> 450000).
     • property_state: two-letter uppercase if present; if absent, leave blank.
     • loan_purpose: map to one of {Purchase, Refinance, HELOC} (case-insensitive). If absent or other, set "Other".
     • phone: digits only (remove spaces, dashes, parentheses). If absent, leave blank.
     • credit_score: if absent, blank, or "Unknown" (case-insensitive), leave blank; otherwise keep as-is (e.g., "720").
   - missing_fields: a semicolon-separated list of any of these that are missing after parsing (use these field names exactly): customer_name, email, phone, loan_purpose, property_state, credit_score, loan_amount. Include a field name in missing_fields if its value is blank in the output.
   - Treat each file independently and set source_file to the filename (e.g., "lead1.txt"). Do not hardcode file names; discover all .txt files in input/inbox/ at runtime.

2) Rewrites every .txt file in input/drafts/ for clarity and brevity and saves results to outputs/rewritten_drafts/ with the same filename. Do not hardcode file names; discover all .txt files in input/drafts/ at runtime.
   - If the first line starts with "Subject:", preserve that line unchanged.
   - For the body (lines after the subject, or all lines if there is no subject):
     • Apply these replacements (case-insensitive on match text, but use the replacement casing as written):
       - "pls" -> "please"
       - "ASAP" -> "as soon as possible"
       - "thx" -> "thank you"
       - "btw" -> "by the way"
       - Remove the phrase "kindly note that"
       - Remove the word "just" when used as filler (e.g., "just wanted")
     • Collapse multiple spaces into a single space and trim leading/trailing whitespace per line.
     • Limit the body to no more than 120 words total; if longer, truncate by words to 120.
   - Append two newlines followed by the exact contents of input/signature.txt at the end of each rewritten file (unless the exact signature text is already present at the end).

Deliverables:
- outputs/applications.csv with the required header and one row per inbox file.
- outputs/applications.jsonl with one JSON object per inbox file containing the same fields as the CSV header.
- outputs/rewritten_drafts/<same filenames>.txt for each draft.

Please make sure the script can be run locally without any external services and that it creates the outputs/ directories if they don't exist. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
